adds.rationale: The profile names 'geopolitical realignment driving energy prices' as an active non-tech wave to watch; Russia's Feb 24 invasion of Ukraine is the in-window trigger that turned this from a watch into a position, and the watchlist currently has zero direct fossil-energy exposure (URA captures only the uranium-via-Rosatom-sanctions leg). XLE is the cleanest, most liquid US-listed proxy: the Energy Select Sector SPDR ETF (launched 1998, ~$1B+ ADV) is market-cap-weighted into XOM and CVX (>40% combined), so one slot at cap=5 buys the integrated-oil-major reflex to $100+ Brent and the Biden Mar 8 executive order banning Russian oil/gas/coal imports. Not in profile exclusions (only solar/wind are excluded).

news_evidence.summary: WTI crude touched $133.46/bbl and Brent reached $139.13/bbl on Mar 7, 2022 — the highest oil price since July 2008 — after Russia's invasion of Ukraine and supply-disruption fears. WTI had started January 2022 around $76/bbl, so this is a ~75% repricing in roughly two months and a structural geopolitical premium, not a transient spike.
news_evidence.source: U.S. Energy Information Administration / EIA Today in Energy

news_evidence.summary: President Biden signed an Executive Order on Mar 8, 2022 banning U.S. imports of Russian oil, liquefied natural gas, and coal, formalizing the Western-energy decoupling from Russia and locking in a structural tailwind for non-Russian fossil producers — exactly the integrated-major exposure XLE concentrates into.

Exxon Mobil (XLE's #1 holding) signaled a ~$2.9B Q1 2022 earnings bump on higher oil prices and announced a planned exit from the Sakhalin-1 Russia joint venture, confirming that the realized-price tailwind was already flowing through to integrated-major P&L within the quarter rather than as a future expectation.

removes.rationale: At cap=5 the geopolitical-energy slot can only be opened by removing one current name, and MSFT is the lowest-conviction holding relative to its thesis. The profile says ride AI but trim before crest, and at cap=5 the watchlist does not need two mega-cap AI anchors — NVDA is the more concentrated AI-compute pure-play (every hyperscaler's training cluster is its silicon), while MSFT's Q1-2022 narrative is dominated by the Jan 18 announcement of a $68.7B all-cash deal to acquire Activision Blizzard, a gaming-consolidation story carrying material FTC/CMA regulatory risk and a multi-year close window — not an AI catalyst. Keeping NVDA preserves the AI anchor; rotating MSFT into XLE adds the live geopolitical-energy wave the profile names verbatim. URA, RKLB, IONQ stay — each is the sole watchlist representative of a profile-named next wave (nuclear, rockets, quantum) with a fresh in-window catalyst (uranium Mar 10 spike to $60+/lb on Rosatom sanction fears; Neutron Wallops/MARS site announcement Feb 28; IONQ Forte ytterbium-system roadmap).

news_evidence.summary: Microsoft announced on Jan 18, 2022 a $95.00-per-share all-cash agreement to acquire Activision Blizzard for ~$68.7B, expected to close in MSFT's fiscal year ending June 30, 2023. The deal is a gaming/Xbox consolidation story, not an AI catalyst, and the long regulatory close window introduces deal-break risk that is not a tailwind to the AI thesis.
news_evidence.source: SEC 8-K / Activision Blizzard / Microsoft press release

news_evidence.summary: Profile-driven rotation: at cap=5 the live geopolitical-energy wave (named verbatim in investor_profile.md as a non-tech wave to watch) has no representative in the current watchlist, and Russia's Feb 24 invasion plus Biden's Mar 8 Russian-energy import ban make Q1 2022 the entry point. NVDA alone is sufficient AI-anchor exposure at this cap, so the swap is MSFT out, XLE in.

rationale_overall: One swap this quarter: MSFT out, XLE in. The profile names geopolitical realignment driving energy prices as an active non-tech wave; the current watchlist [MSFT, NVDA, URA, RKLB, IONQ] had zero direct fossil-energy exposure when Russia's Feb 24 invasion of Ukraine and Biden's Mar 8 Russian-oil/gas/coal import ban repriced Brent from ~$95 to a $139 intraday peak in five weeks. XLE is the cleanest one-slot proxy at cap=5: a 1998-listed, US-listed, deeply liquid ETF that is ~40% XOM+CVX and captures the integrated-major reflex to the $100+ regime that XOM itself was already signaling a $2.9B Q1 earnings bump from. The remove is MSFT, not URA/RKLB/IONQ, because (a) URA already has a fresh dated catalyst (uranium $60+/lb spike on Mar 10 over Rosatom sanction fears) and is the sole nuclear/uranium proxy; (b) RKLB has the Feb 28 Wallops/MARS Neutron site announcement and is the sole rocket proxy; (c) IONQ is the sole publicly-traded pure-play quantum name; while (d) at cap=5 the watchlist does not need two mega-cap AI anchors — NVDA is the more concentrated AI-compute pure-play, and MSFT's dominant Q1-2022 catalyst is the $68.7B Activision acquisition (gaming consolidation with regulatory risk, not AI). Resulting watchlist: NVDA, URA, RKLB, IONQ, XLE — one bet in each of four profile-named waves (AI, nuclear, rockets, quantum) plus the live geopolitical-energy non-tech wave. No engineered-biology add (profile excludes it). No defense add (LMT/RTX have a clean Russia/Ukraine story via the mid-Mar $13.6B supplemental and Javelin replenishment, but defense is not in the profile's named wave list, so the single available slot goes to the explicitly-named geopolitical-energy wave instead).
